paragraphs.body: What is something you can call unpredictable? The best answer to this question is life. Yes, life you heard me right. You walk out of your house thinking you’ll buy a couple of groceries and then return home. But there’s a chance you might never come back. That’s how unpredictable life can be.

Recently, I came across an article by Joseph Holmes titled, “Good night Malaysia 370”. What interested me more was not the article, but the title of the article itself. These were the last words said by the captain of that aircraft. He then goes on to explain what lead to this horrific plane crash and how millions and millions of dollars were spent on a search which led to nothing. Alright, let’s get back to the words “Good night” said by the pilot. The pilot seems to be very calm and wishing the people back at the control tower a pleasant night. Less he knows that him and the two ninety-eight people onboard will be sleeping forever in a matter of few minutes.

annotations.comment: try the templates and writing moves for summary that we learned about!

paragraphs.body: The question remains: why is life so unpredictable? The simplest way I can answer is that we can’t control everything that happens to us. I remember when I was in middle school; I used to take my exams very seriously and studied to achieve the best possible scores. Sometimes, I would not get the desired results, and other times I would. I concluded that life’s unpredictability can lead to unexpected outcomes, which can be either good or bad. Often, people might say these outcomes can be controlled, which may be true in some scenarios. For example, cramming the day before an exam might lead to a poor outcome, while those who study regularly may have a completely different experience. Looking at this from a different angle, there are some outcomes that cannot be controlled. The recent news about the Baltimore bridge collapsing—who knew this was going to happen? No one, literally no one. The people on the bridge, who were on their way to work, listening to the radio and having a good time, were completely unprepared for this collapse.

annotations.text: The recent news about the Baltimore bridge collapsing—who knew this was going to happen? No one, literally no one. The people on the bridge, who were on their way to work, listening to the radio and having a good time, were completely unprepared for this collapse.

paragraphs.body: Who knew the people on the Titanic, who were on vacation, would never return? Who knew the people on Flight 370, who were on their way to meet their loved ones, would never return? This is how unpredictable life can be. Some people might call this terrifying, but it’s simply how life works, I guess. You literally cannot predict your tomorrow. While some might try to forecast what profession, they will have ten years from now, they often ignore the reality of life’s unpredictability.

After going over Holmes article about flight 370 crash, I was able to form a link between the captains last words which were “Good night Malaysia 370” and life’s unpredictability. According to Holmes, “The story of MH370 is one of failures and broken hopes: from accusations that the aircraft should never have been cleared to fly; to the heartbroken families who took the investigation into their own hands and may even have found a piece of the plane; to the mishaps by the airline during the vital first few hours”. Holmes point is that sometimes these unpredictable outcomes can be a result of human error which can lead to unbearable losses. The innocent lives of 298 people on board were lost for no reason. Could we have avoided this outcome? Absolutely, we could have if the airline had made a timely decision to prevent that plane from taking off. Holmes further states in his article that, “In the initial hours of the crash, families endured false hopes, unsubstantiated rumors and shocks as they waited for news of their loved ones.” Basically, Holmes is trying to convey the message that in such incidents, due to human error, families are the ones who must endure the most pain. It’s not even their fault, yet they still must go through such a difficult phase. Another point to note is that, although we might call this incident unpredictable and suggest it could have been controlled, let me remind you that the idea of control is often an illusion.

annotations.text: According to Holmes, “The story of MH370 is one of failures and broken hopes: from accusations that the aircraft should never have been cleared to fly; to the heartbroken families who took the investigation into their own hands and may even have found a piece of the plane; to the mishaps by the airline during the vital first few hours”. Holmes point is that sometimes these unpredictable outcomes can be a result of human error which can lead to unbearable losses.

paragraphs.body: When I was a kid, I remember watching those Final Destination movies where unpredictable incidents would occur, leading to people losing their lives. Two scenes that stand out to me are the plane crash and the roller coaster falling off the track. Honestly, the roller coaster scene happened because someone brought their camera on the ride, even though the sign clearly said, “No such items allowed.” Therefore, another human error caused this deadly incident to occur.

annotations.text: Therefore, another human error caused this deadly incident to occur.

paragraphs.body: As I said in the previous paragraph, the word control is often an illusion. Unpredictability has existed and it will exist until the very end. We can’t control it, but we can change it with the decisions we make every day. Any bad decision can lead to something very unpleasant and the opposite for every good decision. If we start making good decisions today, I’m pretty sure we will never have regrets in our lives. This unpredictability in our lives can bring both excitement and anxiety, pushing us to adapt and discover new possibilities in our lives. While we may plan for our future and anticipate different outcomes for ourselves, unpredictable events can shift our paths either in a good direction or a bad direction. What our aim should be is that we should make ourselves resilient to these types of unpredictable changes and let them be a learning lesson for us.

annotations.text: If we start making good decisions today, I’m pretty sure we will never have regrets in our lives. This unpredictability in our lives can bring both excitement and anxiety, pushing us to adapt and discover new possibilities in our lives.

paragraphs.body: Life might be unpredictable, but that unpredictability can sometimes come in the form of good times. These are the events you should celebrate and appreciate. You should try to cherish every bit of these moments, as they can bring joy when least expected. It might be a fun day with friends or a special surprise from a loved one. These little joys can brighten our days and lift our spirits. In the end, life is indeed unpredictable, and it’s those joyful surprises that remind us to embrace every experience fully and to stay open to the happiness that comes our way.
